name: Western New York Independent Living, Inc. (WNYIL)
summary: The anchor Center for Independent Living for the Buffalo/Erie area and lead of a family of disability-run agencies serving Western New York.
source_notes: First-party site. Anchor partner (§3). disabled_led=true rests on the federal Center for Independent Living definition: CILs are consumer-controlled (>=51% of staff/board are people with disabilities). Address confirmed on the NYSILC ILC directory. NEEDS OWNER/ORG SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING (no proof).

name: Independent Living of Niagara County (ILNC)
summary: A peer-run independent living center serving people with disabilities across Niagara County.
source_notes: First-party WNYIL family-of-agencies page. Peer-run ILC; disabled_led per CIL consumer-control federal requirement. Niagara County (adjacent to the Erie beachhead). NEEDS SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING.

name: Independent Living of the Genesee Region (ILGR)
summary: A peer-directed disability civil-rights organization serving Genesee, Wyoming, and Orleans Counties (also has an Albion office at 243 South Main Street, Albion 14411).
source_notes: First-party WNYIL page. Self-describes as 'a grassroots, peer directed, multi-cultural civil rights organization for/of people with disabilities.' disabled_led per CIL consumer-control. NEEDS SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING.

name: Mental Health Peer Connection (MHPC)
summary: A peer-run behavioral-health organization providing advocacy, peer counseling, and independent-living support, run for and by people with behavioral health disabilities.
source_notes: First-party WNYIL page. Explicitly 'an organization run for and by people with behavioral health disabilities' — disabled_led directly stated. WNYIL affiliate; shares the 3108 Main St address. NEEDS SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING.

name: Southwestern Independent Living Center, Inc. (SILC)
summary: A nonresidential nonprofit Center for Independent Living serving people with disabilities throughout Chautauqua County.
source_notes: NYSILC official ILC directory + first-party site (silcchq.org). Designated NYS CIL, consumer-controlled per federal definition. NEEDS SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING.

name: Directions in Independent Living (DIL)
summary: A Center for Independent Living serving Cattaraugus and Allegany Counties, with a satellite office in Wellsville (55 E. Fassett Lane, Wellsville 14895).
source_notes: First-party site + NYSILC ILC directory. Designated NYS CIL, consumer-controlled per federal definition. NEEDS SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING.

name: New York Association on Independent Living (NYAIL)
summary: The statewide membership association of New York's Independent Living Centers, advocating for the independent living and rights of New Yorkers with disabilities.
source_notes: First-party site. STATEWIDE ANCHOR — reference/context only, not WNY-local; do not seed deep. disabled_led leans on its CIL-membership structure; the About page does not itself assert a disability-majority board — CONFIRM before publishing that flag.

name: People Inc. Health Services Building (with ECMC Center for Dental Care)
summary: A health and dental center in North Buffalo built around serving people with developmental disabilities, with a dental wheelchair-tilt lift and abundant accessible parking.
source_notes: Developer (Uniland) page + People Inc. corroboration. Accessible-parking evidence exists ('abundant patient parking and a designated drop-off under a porte-cochere') BUT the schema's accessible_parking attribute is place-only (applies_to_kind='place'), so it cannot attach to this provider record — recorded here as a note pending the schema fix in gaps.md (make accessible_parking apply to both kinds). Co-located ECMC dental clinic serves people with developmental disabilities and has a wheelchair-tilt lift (dental positioning, NOT a medical exam table — no key). Secondary source; recruit for MDE photo-verification.

name: ECMC Center for Dental Care — Special Needs Dentistry
summary: Erie County Medical Center's special-needs dental program serving people with intellectual/developmental disabilities, stroke/TBI, and dementia, in-clinic or under general anesthesia.
source_notes: ECMC (public hospital) program page states it welcomes individuals with intellectual/developmental disabilities, stroke/TBI history, and dementia, and offers full-mouth rehab under general anesthesia. Disability-competent care explicitly stated; no MDE/exam-table claim — assert none.

name: Jericho Road Community Health Center — Doat Street Clinic
summary: A Buffalo FQHC clinic location listed as handicap accessible with accessible entrance, restrooms, and parking.

attributes.note: 211WNY directory: 'Accessible entrance, restrooms, and parking spaces.'
211WNY directory: 'Accessible entrance, restrooms, and parking spaces.'

source_notes: 211WNY (United Way) directory: 'Accessible entrance, restrooms, and parking spaces.' FQHC — HHS-funded, relevant to the July 8 2026 MDE deadline. NOTE: the source also states accessible PARKING, but the schema's accessible_parking attribute is place-only (applies_to_kind='place') and cannot attach to a provider — held as a note pending the schema fix in gaps.md. Physical-access fields are directory-reported, not disability-specific care; disability_literate left false. Strong recruit; verify MDE (exam table, scale) via photo.

name: Neighborhood Health Center — Northwest
summary: A Buffalo FQHC offering primary, dental, and specialty care, listed as handicap accessible.

attributes.note: 211WNY directory: 'Handicap Accessible? Yes.' A GENERAL flag, not an entrance-specific claim — mapped conservatively; re-verify the entrance specifically.

source_notes: 211WNY directory. FQHC (HHS-funded — July 8 2026 MDE deadline). Only a general 'Handicap Accessible? Yes' flag published. disability_literate false; recruit for MDE verification.

name: Neighborhood Health Center — Blasdell
summary: A suburban Erie County FQHC location listed as handicap accessible.

attributes.note: 211WNY directory: 'Handicap Accessible? Yes.' General flag only — re-verify the entrance specifically.

source_notes: 211WNY directory. FQHC (HHS-funded — MDE deadline). General handicap-accessible flag only; mapped conservatively. disability_literate false; recruit for MDE verification.

name: Aspire of WNY — Health Care Services (Article 28 Clinic)
summary: An Article 28 medical clinic within a longstanding developmental-disability agency, offering general medical, PT, speech, and seating/positioning services and listed as handicap accessible.

attributes.note: 211WNY directory: handicap accessible = 'Yes.' General flag — re-verify the entrance specifically.

source_notes: 211WNY directory + Aspire site. Aspire (formerly Cerebral Palsy Association of WNY) serves people with developmental disabilities; clinic lists seating/positioning aids, mobility training, AAC, and general medical care — supports disability_literate. Only a general accessible flag; MDE needs photo verification. Address returns as Buffalo 14225 in one field, Cheektowaga in another (14225 is the Cheektowaga ZIP).

name: Evergreen Health
summary: A Buffalo FQHC providing primary, behavioral, and supportive care with a stated non-discrimination policy and disability accommodations including sign-language interpreters.
source_notes: Evergreen's own accessibility/non-discrimination page provides qualified sign-language interpreters and non-discrimination on the basis of disability. disability_literate grounded in communication-access accommodation, NOT any physical-MDE claim (page states no exam-table/scale/entrance detail). FQHC (HHS-funded — MDE deadline). Recruit for physical MDE verification.

name: VIA — Visually Impaired Advancement (Low Vision Clinic)
summary: A Buffalo agency and low-vision clinic providing vision rehabilitation and clinical services for people who are blind or visually impaired; listed as handicap accessible.

attributes.note: 211WNY directory: organization is handicap accessible. General flag — re-verify the entrance specifically.

source_notes: 211WNY directory. VIA serves people who are legally blind or visually impaired with low-vision rehabilitation — supports disability_literate (sensory-disability competence). Only a general accessible flag; physical MDE not addressed.

name: Erie County Medical Center (ECMC) — Main Campus
summary: Erie County's public hospital and Level 1 trauma center, recognized for safe patient handling and mobility; a candidate to recruit for documented accessible diagnostic equipment.
source_notes: Public hospital. Recognized by the Association of Safe Patient Handling Professionals (secondary mention) and hosts the special-needs dental program (separate record). No published disability-specific-care or MDE statement on general pages, so disability_literate=false — a recruit tied to the ADA Title II state/local MDE deadline (Aug 9 2026). Verify all attributes before asserting.

name: University at Buffalo — Special Care Dental Clinic (Squire Hall)
street: 3435 Main Street (Squire Hall, UB South Campus)
summary: A UB dental clinic (state-funded, projected to open 2027) purpose-built for people with intellectual/developmental disabilities, featuring a wheelchair tilt, wheelchair-accessible panoramic X-ray, sensory-friendly rooms, and extra-large restrooms.

attributes.note: UB News: 'extra-large restrooms that can better accommodate patient needs.' PLANNED facility (projected open 2027) — forward-looking, not operational; do NOT treat as available.

source_notes: UB News (institutional). Explicitly for individuals with intellectual/developmental disabilities; features a wheelchair tilt, wheelchair-accessible panoramic X-ray, sensory-friendly rooms. IMPORTANT: projected to open 2027 — PLANNED, not operational. Do not surface as available. Strong future disability-competent listing.

name: University at Buffalo / Arc GLOW — S-Miles To Go Mobile Dental Program
street: Arc of Genesee Orleans Community Center, 38 Woodrow Road
summary: A UB-partnered mobile dental clinic serving WNY patients with disabilities from a wheelchair-lift-equipped van, with overflow care at UB's Squire Hall clinic in Erie County.
source_notes: UB News. A 42-foot, three-chair dental van with digital X-ray, sterilization center, and wheelchair lift; UB partnered with Arc of Genesee Orleans to serve patients with disabilities. GEOGRAPHY: home base is Batavia (Genesee County), NOT Erie — included as a WNY disability-dental program that also delivers care at UB's Erie County clinic. Van wheelchair lift is a transport lift, not a facility MDE attribute (note only).

name: Catholic Health (WNY)
summary: A Buffalo-based nonprofit health system offering 24/7 video remote interpreting including ASL across its network; a candidate to recruit for documented accessible diagnostic equipment.
source_notes: PR Newswire (system press release). Uses InDemand video remote interpreting including American Sign Language — supports disability_literate on the Deaf/HoH communication-access dimension only. No physical-MDE statement; recruit for exam-table/scale/entrance verification (Aug 9 2026 deadline). PRESS-RELEASE source — corroborate on Catholic Health's own site before elevating.

name: Service Bridges, Inc.
summary: A Deaf-owned ASL and sign-language interpreting agency serving the Deaf, DeafBlind, and hard-of-hearing community.
source_notes: Business's own About page states it was founded to be an agency 'run by the Deaf'; founder/CEO described as a Deaf man. Also appears in Gallaudet University's Apple Maps 'Deaf-Owned Businesses in Western NY' guide. High reliability for the disabled-owned/led claim (first-party + third-party curated list). disability_literate true (interpreting for the Deaf community is the core service). NEEDS OWNER SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING (no proof).

name: The Hoag Group LLC
summary: A residential and commercial general construction and landscaping firm founded by a service-disabled veteran.
source_notes: NYS OGS-certified SDVOB — highest reliability (state certifies a service-disabled-veteran owner). OGS release lists 'The Hoag Group, Buffalo, N.Y.' Street address not published; resolve at onboarding. disability_literate=false (different axis). Do NOT infer the specific disability from 'service-disabled veteran.' NEEDS OWNER SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING (no proof).

name: Greater Frontier LLC
summary: A construction cost-estimating and project-management firm founded by military veterans.
source_notes: NYS OGS-certified SDVOB — highest reliability. OGS release lists 'Greater Frontier, Buffalo, N.Y.' Company site confirms veteran-founded. No street address published; resolve at onboarding. NEEDS OWNER SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING (no proof).

name: Vanguard Innovative Solutions LLC
summary: A computer systems design and IT services firm owned by a service-disabled veteran.
source_notes: NYS OGS-certified SDVOB — highest reliability. OGS release lists 'Vanguard Innovative Solutions, East Amherst, N.Y.' Address from a NY business filing (may be registered/mailing address) — verify at onboarding. East Amherst is in Erie County. NEEDS OWNER SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING (no proof).

name: Buffalo Veteran Contracting, LLC
summary: A general contracting and excavating business owned by a service-disabled veteran, in Erie County.
source_notes: NYS OGS SDVOB directory (control page above) — highest reliability. West Falls, NY (Erie County) per BBB and NY filings; exact street not surfaced publicly. NEEDS OWNER SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING (no proof).

name: Aveteran Corp.
summary: A self-identified service-disabled-veteran-owned construction firm in Buffalo.
source_notes: Self-listed on veteranownedbusiness.com as a 'verified Service Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Business (SDVOSB)' in construction. MEDIUM reliability — a self-listed directory claim, NOT the NYS OGS cert. Confirm against NYS OGS or the federal VA SDVOSB registry at onboarding. NEEDS OWNER SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING (no proof).

name: CW Snow Plowing
summary: A snow-plowing and landscaping business owned by a service-disabled veteran, in Erie County.
source_notes: NYS OGS-certified SDVOB (Cheektowaga, Erie County). Street address not published; resolve at onboarding. High reliability for the disabled-owned flag (state cert). NEEDS OWNER SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING (no proof).

name: Buffalo AKG Art Museum
summary: Art museum with power-assist accessible entrances, accessible restrooms across both buildings, and first-come accessible parking.

attributes.note: 'Accessible entrances, with power assist doors, are located on both sides of the Knox Building and on the ground and parking level of the Gundlach Building.'
'Accessible/single-user restrooms are located on the entry level of the Knox Building and on the Parking Level Lobby and Floors 1-3 of the Gundlach Building.'
'Accessible parking is available at the loop entrance by the Knox Building... first-come, first-served.'

source_notes: First-party museum accessibility page. High reliability.

name: The Buffalo History Museum
summary: History museum with a wheelchair-accessible side entrance (with an approach incline), accessible restrooms on two levels, and free accessible parking.

attributes.note: 'Our wheelchair-accessible entrance is located beyond the marble retaining wall, on the right side of the Museum.' CAUTION: source notes an incline to approach this side entrance; an elevator serves all three levels.
'There are wheelchair-accessible restrooms located on the main level and on the ground level.'
'The Buffalo History Museum offers free and accessible parking in the Museum's adjacent lot.'

source_notes: First-party museum accessibility page. High reliability.

name: Buffalo Museum of Science
summary: Science museum with accessible parking near the entrance and gender-neutral bathrooms with changing tables on multiple floors.

attributes.note: 'Museum parking is located on the side of the building facing Humboldt Parkway with accessible parking spaces available closer to the entrance.'

source_notes: First-party accessibility page. High reliability. Step-free entrance and accessible restroom NOT explicitly asserted by the source, so omitted (page mentions free wheelchairs and gender-neutral bathrooms but not an explicitly accessible restroom or step-free entrance).

name: Shea's Buffalo Theatre (Shea's Performing Arts Center)
summary: Historic theater whose main level is wheelchair accessible via the Main Street entrances; as a National Historic Site it has no elevator or balcony access and is not fully ADA compliant.

attributes.note: 'Shea's Buffalo Theatre's main level is wheelchair accessible.' Enter via the Main Street entrances. CAUTION: venue self-describes as not fully ADA compliant; no elevator, main level only, restrooms on the second floor.

source_notes: First-party plan-your-visit page. High reliability, but venue self-describes as not fully ADA compliant (no elevator, main level only). Accessible restroom and parking NOT asserted, so omitted.

name: Explore & More — The Ralph C. Wilson, Jr. Children's Museum
summary: Children's museum at Canalside with a recommended step-free entrance via an accessibility ramp and adjoining bridge.

attributes.note: Museum recommends the Commercial Street entrance via an accessibility ramp and adjoining bridge. CAUTION: drawn from a search-cached excerpt of the museum's own page — direct fetch returned HTTP 403; re-fetch the first-party page to confirm.

source_notes: First-party page, but WebFetch was blocked (403); attribute is from the search engine's cached excerpt. MEDIUM reliability pending re-fetch. Accessible restroom/parking not asserted, so omitted.

'There are three handicapped parking meters located around the Central Library building' (Oak, William, and Clinton Streets).

source_notes: First-party library directions/accessibility page. High reliability. Automatic doors noted in the entrance evidence (no dedicated schema key). Accessible restroom not mentioned, so omitted.

name: NFTA Metro Rail — University Station
street: Main Street at UB South Campus
summary: Above-ground northern terminus of Metro Rail with an elevated platform and handrails for accessible boarding.

attributes.note: 'All Metro Rail trains and stations are accessible to riders with disabilities.' Elevated platforms with handrails at each above-ground station. CAUTION: wheelchair riders must board the frontmost door of the front car — a system-wide claim, not a per-station physical audit.

source_notes: First-party NFTA-Metro accessibility page (system-wide statement covering all stations). High reliability for the system-wide claim; per-station specifics not individually detailed. Restroom/parking omitted.

name: NFTA Metro Rail — Canalside Station
summary: Above-ground downtown terminus of Metro Rail with an elevated platform and handrails for accessible boarding.

attributes.note: 'All Metro Rail trains and stations are accessible to riders with disabilities.' Elevated platforms with handrails at each above-ground station. CAUTION: board the frontmost car; system-wide claim.

source_notes: First-party NFTA-Metro accessibility page (system-wide statement). High reliability for the system-wide claim; per-station specifics not detailed. Restroom/parking omitted.

name: NFTA Metro Rail — Fountain Plaza Station
summary: Downtown at-grade Metro Rail station covered by NFTA's system-wide accessibility commitment.

attributes.note: 'All Metro Rail trains and stations are accessible to riders with disabilities.' CAUTION: board the frontmost door of the front car; system-wide claim, not a per-station audit.

source_notes: First-party NFTA-Metro accessibility page (system-wide statement). High reliability for the system-wide claim only. Restroom/parking omitted.

name: NFTA Metro Rail — Lafayette Square Station
summary: Downtown underground Metro Rail station served by elevators under NFTA's system-wide accessibility commitment.

attributes.note: 'All Metro Rail trains and stations are accessible to riders with disabilities.' Underground stations reached via elevators, escalators, and stairs. CAUTION: elevator-dependent underground station; riders can use the 'Call for Aid' PACE panel if an elevator is out of service — elevator outages are a real freeze-worthy risk (§4 favor dissent on safety).

source_notes: First-party NFTA-Metro accessibility page (system-wide statement). High reliability for the claim; per-station elevator status not live-verified. Restroom/parking omitted.

name: Tifft Nature Preserve
summary: Nature preserve on the Outer Harbor with a project to make its trail loop wheelchair/walker/stroller friendly; free visitor parking.

attributes.note: Reporting notes ADA parking-lot access improvements and free parking in the Visitor lot. CAUTION: the accessible-trail rebuild (wheelchair-friendly loop, rebuilt Heritage Boardwalk) was described as a planned/in-progress project — do NOT assert a finished step-free trail without re-confirming completion.

source_notes: First-party preserve page, but content describes a project in progress rather than completed access facts. MEDIUM reliability — re-verify trail/boardwalk completion before asserting step-free entrance. Only parking asserted.

name: Chestnut Ridge Park
summary: Large Erie County park with a fully-accessible playground, accessible fishing pier, ADA-repaved casino lot, and a family restroom with an adult-size changing table.

attributes.note: Erie County Parks page: the casino parking lot was repaved and 'upgraded to be ADA compliant.'
'an adult-size changing table in our Family Restroom' at the Chestnut Ridge Casino.

source_notes: First-party Erie County Parks accessibility-and-inclusion page. High reliability. Step-free entrance not asserted for a specific building (park is largely outdoor grounds), so omitted.

name: Ellicott Creek Park
summary: Erie County Heritage Park with an accessible playground and, park-wide, universal picnic tables that accommodate wheelchairs.

attributes.note: Accessible playground completed (Summer 2023) and park-wide universal picnic tables. CAUTION: 'accessible parking' here is a general park-access claim; the page does not separately enumerate designated accessible spaces at this specific park — treat conservatively.

source_notes: First-party Erie County Parks page. Medium-high reliability — accessible-playground and universal-picnic-table facts are explicit; a designated accessible-parking claim for this specific park is not individually stated. Restroom/entrance omitted.

name: Emery Park
summary: Erie County Heritage Park with an accessible playground completed as part of the county's five-park accessibility initiative.
source_notes: First-party Erie County Parks page names Emery Park among the five Heritage Parks that received accessible playgrounds by Summer 2023. Listed for coverage; no specific step-free-entrance / accessible-restroom / accessible-parking attribute is individually asserted for this park, so attributes are intentionally empty rather than fabricated.

name: Fly By Cafe LLC
summary: A breakfast cafe and bed-and-breakfast run by a service-disabled veteran.
source_notes: NYS OGS-certified SDVOB — highest reliability (state certifies a service-disabled-veteran owner). SCHEMA GAP: this is a 'place' (cafe/B&B) but provider_profiles.disabled_owned/led attaches only to 'provider' listings — so the disabled_owned/led flags are recorded at TOP LEVEL here with no home in the current schema (see gaps.md). Do NOT infer the specific disability from 'service-disabled veteran.' Niagara County (adjacent to the Erie beachhead). NEEDS OWNER SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING (no proof).
